subject: HIST
requirements: Humanities and Arts with Race & Ethnicity(*)
course_description: This course is an introduction to the Middle Ages (500 to 1500 CE) and Europe's place in the world. It provides a survey of interactions with pagans (Germanic tribes and the Vikings) and the Byzantine, Islamic, and Mongol empires that shaped medieval society.
course_notes: This course is an introduction to the Middle Ages (500 to 1500 CE) and Europe's place in the world. It provides a survey of interactions with pagans and the Byzantine and Islamic empires that shaped medieval society, particularly how medieval Europeans viewed the world and why they believed themselves to be religiously and culturally superior. Special attention will be given to the enslavement of people, the Muslim and Jewish communities living within medieval Europe, and European involvement in the Mediterranean through the crusades, conquest, and trade. The history of Muslim-Christian relations in this region meant that contact facilitated the spread of ideas, food, technology, art, and architecture from the Islamic world to Europe. We will read accounts of Christian merchants who traveled throughout the Middle East and to the Mongol empire in Central Asia. Other highlights of the course include forced conversions, knightly culture, the power of queens and noblewomen, religious devotion, monasticism and the military orders. This course has a light reading load.
